title: MaybeUninit dummy params for error-path tests: correct and the right trade-off
description: Error-path tests that fire before builder.build() (NoSpendableNotes, DustAmount, etc.) use an uninitialized MaybeUninit<(SpendParameters, OutputParameters)> and assume_init_ref() to avoid depending on the 48MB Sapling params files. The safety argument holds: MaybeUninit<T> has no Drop impl, so no destructor UB; assume_init_ref() creates a reference that is never used to access any field or call any method before the early return. Rejected alternatives: (a) skip-if-no-params — creates a false runtime dependency on 48MB files for logic that tests zero proof code; (b) unsafe zeroed() — Arc fields in params would crash on drop because the refcount pointer would be null/invalid. A contributor seeing 'unsafe' here might suggest 'just use Option' (wrong: the signature requires &(SpendParameters, OutputParameters)) or 'download the files' (wrong: 48MB downloads in unit tests are not acceptable).
close_reason: decision records documented in beads; no code change required
